title: Collapsed Victoria Street building site handed back to owner
description: GEORGE NEWS - The building site in Victoria...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The building site in Victoria Street where the Neo Victoria apartment block collapsed on 6 May was returned to the owner last week after the police and Department of Employment and Labour completed their investigation of the site.


> Several investigations were launched, among others by the Western Cape Government, the police and the department.


Southern Cape police spokesperson Sergeant Chris Spies said an inquest docket had been opened after the collapse. He said that the police had finished their investigation at the site itself.

"An inquest docket was opened at the time of the incident when the first victim was retrieved from the debris. This investigation is still underway. The investigation at the construction site was concluded.

"Evidence gathered at the site forms part of the ongoing police investigation. Meanwhile, all 34 deceased were formally identified and the last body was handed to the next of kin on Monday 10 June," said Spies.


> No time frame could be given for completion of the investigation.


The provincial chief inspector for the Department of Employment and Labour, David Esau, said that there was no need to continue work at the site itself, and it had been handed over to the landowner.

He added that it is difficult to estimate when the department's probe would be completed because investigators were struggling to access people who still needed to be interviewed.

"We are currently busy with the investigation and because of the number of people to be interviewed we cannot attach any time frames."

He said the department was considering asking investigating officers in those countries to visit the people, but it could also request that they return to South Africa.


> Wouter Kriel, spokesperson for the Department of Local Government, said at this stage no new information is available regarding the engineer's investigation, which is ongoing.


The head of communications at George Municipality, Chantèl Edwards, said the municipality is not responsible for the investigation into the cause of the collapse and is not privy to the outcomes of the criminal and health and safety investigations undertaken by the police and Labour Department.

Asked about appeals from community members that the land should become a public memorial garden, developer Neotrend's attorney, Ryan du Preez, responded, "We can confirm that there has been a cooperative approach among all parties involved in the project and that they are working closely together to determine the best path forward for the site.


> "While specific details are still being finalised, we give the assurance that more information will be shared with the community in due course."


Pervious articles:

- George pays tribute to Victoria Street victims
- Death toll 34, 18 formally identified
- Update: 47 victims linked to their families
- President Cyril Ramaphosa coming to George tomorrow
- Update: Death toll remains 33 while 19 still missing
- Update: One family finds closure
- Building collapse: 19 (out of 81) still missing

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
